question_text: What is the difference between mass and weight?
correct_answer: Mass is the amount of matter in an object and is measured in kilograms. Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object and is measured in newtons.
explanation: Mass stays the same wherever you are, but weight changes depending on the strength of gravity. For example, your mass on Earth is the same as on the Moon, but you would weigh less on the Moon because gravity is weaker there.
